implementationPrerequisites: Expose a deterministic mastered-weapon count from WeaponProficiency.
Set mastered=true on the initial try_emplace path when capped XP reaches max experience.
Choose an explicit existing-player backfill point after load normalization.
Use PlayerAchievement::add for idempotent canonical ID awards.
Prove every ID 567 weapon item ID and resolved proficiency eligibility before adding the definition or hook.

scenarios.title: Existing entry reaches first mastery
setup: Player has one tracked weapon at maxExperience-1.
actions: Award one proficiency XP through the normal addExperience path.
expected: Weapon is mastered.
Mastered count is 1.
Achievement 564 is awarded once.
Achievements 565 and 566 remain locked.

scenarios.title: Initial entry reaches mastery in one gain
setup: Weapon has no stored proficiency entry.
Gain is greater than or equal to maxExperience.
expected: Stored XP is capped at maxExperience.
mastered is true before return.
Mastered count includes the weapon.
Threshold awards are evaluated.

scenarios.title: Mastery thresholds 1, 10 and 50
setup: Prepare players with 0, 9 and 49 mastered weapons respectively.
actions: Master one additional eligible weapon for each player.
expected: ID 564 unlocks at count 1.
ID 565 unlocks at count 10.
ID 566 unlocks at count 50.
No threshold unlocks early.

scenarios.title: Idempotent repeated evaluation
setup: Player already owns threshold achievements and has qualifying mastery counts.
actions: Re-evaluate thresholds after additional XP, relog and explicit backfill.
expected: No duplicate points or messages.
Unlocked timestamps are not replaced unless existing API specifies otherwise.

scenarios.title: Existing-player load backfill
setup: Persist 1, 10 and 50 max-XP weapon entries without threshold achievements.
actions: Load player and normalize stored proficiency state.
expected: mastered flags are derived from XP.
Threshold achievements are awarded exactly once at the selected post-load integration point.
No gameplay action is required to repair historical state.

scenarios.title: Stale mastered flag normalization
setup: Persist maxExperience with mastered=false and below-max XP with mastered=true.
expected: mastered reflects normalized XP state.
Only normalized mastered entries contribute to counts.

scenarios.title: Persistence and relog
setup: Master a weapon and unlock a threshold achievement.
actions: Save, logout, reload and reopen Weapon Proficiency UI.
expected: XP, mastered state, selected perks and achievement remain consistent.
No duplicate award occurs on reload.

scenarios.title: Catalyst and combat parity
setup: Use separate weapons one XP step below mastery.
actions: Master one through combat XP and one through Player:addWeaponExperience catalyst flow.
expected: Both paths pass through the same transition and threshold evaluation.

scenarios.title: The Forbidden Build item eligibility contract
setup: Provide the reviewed canonical item ID set for all reference names.
Each item has a resolved nonzero valid proficiency ID.
actions: Master each required candidate independently and evaluate the secret condition.
expected: ID 567 is not implemented until all item IDs and eligibility are proven.
After implementation, only the exact reviewed set satisfies the secret condition.
Items sharing a name or lacking active proficiency do not count by accident.

scenarios.title: Invalid or removed proficiency data
setup: Persist an invalid item ID, an item with proficiencyId=0 and a removed proficiency definition.
actions: Load player and evaluate mastery achievements.
expected: Invalid entries are skipped by existing validation.
They do not increase mastered count or unlock achievements.
